channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: The American woman who went viral in Pakistan has a crypto coin
item.description: The “American woman in Pakistan” now has a crypto coin. If you don’t know who that is, American Onijah Andrew Robinson recently went viral after claiming she flew to Pakistan to marry a 19-year-old she met online, only to be rejected.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: DOGE has disregarded data protection and privacy norms. The consequences will be felt years down the line
item.description: It has been a tumultuous few weeks since Donald Trump took office for the second time as president of the United States, While Trump has garnered headlines for his outlandish executive orders aimed at recasting the country in his own shape, Elon Musk, the tech billionaire turned government quango-bu
item.dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker, Fast Company

item.title: How to strengthen the security of Oracle applications without relying on OAU
item.description: Vendor support agreements have long been a sticking point for customers, and the Oracle Applications Unlimited (OAU) program is no different.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: On average, state cyber leaders are leaving their positions sooner
item.description: The National Association of State CIOs and Deloitte outlined five calls to action in a recent survey of state chief information security officers. The post On average, state cyber leaders are leaving their positions sooner first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: How Elon Musk $97.4 billion bid complicates matters for OpenAI
item.description: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman has dismissed a $97.4 billion takeover bid led by rival Elon Musk, but the unsolicited offer could complicate Altman’s push to transform the maker of ChatGPT into a for-profit company.
item.dc:creator: Matt O'Brien, Thalia Beaty And Kelvin Chan, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: France set to pledge one gigawatt of nuclear power for AI
item.description: France will add even more nuclear energy to its portfolio to power the next generation of AI data centers.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: How Elon Musk’s crusade against government could benefit Tesla
item.description: The Trump administration has not yet declared any actions that could benefit Tesla or Musk’s other companies. However, snuffing out federal investigations or jettisoning safety initiatives would be an easier task than their assault on regulators and the bureaucracy.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: The 70mai Omni 4K Dash Cam: A Comprehensive Upgrade for Your Vehicle
item.description: Discover the latest upgrade from 70mai, featuring 4K/60p video support, a rotating main camera, and dual-channel recording capabilities, all starting at $279.99/£268.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Microsoft gets rid of ‘Edge uninstall’ advice page after facing criticism over it having nothing to do with removing the app, and just promoting the browser instead
item.description: Microsoft has made good headway with improving Edge in recent times, but it needs to stop with the promotional activity, particularly the weirder stuff.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Marvel Rivals The Thing and Human Torch release date
item.description: Here's when The Thing and Human Torch are being added toMarvel Rivals. We'll also give some of our predictions for how each character will work.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: MSI mistakenly fires up a ‘countdown’ to Nvidia RTX 5070 Ti GPU release that suggests February 20 launch rumors could be true
item.description: Can’t wait for Nvidia’s next Blackwell graphics card? The RTX 5070 Ti could be here soon, but worryingly, there’s still no chatter about the RTX 5070.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: JD Vance rails against 'excessive' AI regulation in a rebuke to Europe at the Paris AI summit
item.description: U_S_ Vice President JD Vance has told a Paris summit on artificial intelligence that the Trump administration will “ensure that AI systems developed in America are free from ideological bias,” and that the U_S_ would “never restrict our citizens’ right to free speech.”.
item.dc:creator: Aamer Madhani And Thomas Adamson, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: VPN vs. ZTNA: Cisco tackles pros and cons
item.description: It’s no secret that more modern approaches to remote access have been usurping VPNs as organizations adapt to the realities of a distributed workforce, cloud-based applications, and heightened security threats.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: OpenAI shouldn’t accept Elon Musk’s $97 billion bid to buy it
item.description: Let’s say you own one of the most valuable homes in a lush, gated community that has been earmarked as a future point of growth for decades to come. One day, a letter appears in your mailbox, offering to buy your property for between a third and two-thirds of its value on the open market.
item.dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker, Fast Company

item.title: Ukraine's rare earth elements could help keep US military aid flowing
item.description: Ukraine has offered to strike a deal with U.S. President Donald Trump for continued American military aid in exchange for developing Ukraine’s mineral industry.
item.dc:creator: Samya Kullab, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: C++, Go, and Rust gaining popularity – Tiobe
item.description: With Tiobe’s February 2025 rankings, the publisher of the monthly index of programming language popularity notes that fast programming languages such as C++, Go, and Rust are gaining ground.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: News influencers favored Trump over Harris during campaign, study finds
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Americans who followed news influencers during the presidential campaign were more likely to hear positive reports about Donald Trump than they were about Kamala Harris, a study has revealed.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Cisco launches AI Renewals Agent with Mistral AI
item.description: Cisco rolled out its first of many AI agents codeveloped with partner Mistral AI, a genAI and large language model (LLM) startup.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Facebook owner Meta confirms thousands of layoffs it previously revealed
item.description: Facebook app owner Meta Platforms said it has launched worldwide job cuts potentially affecting thousands of workers.
item.dc:creator: George Avalos, The Mercury News

item.title: Google’s latest genAI shift is a reminder to IT leaders
item.description: Every enterprise CIO knows they cannot — and should not — ever trust a vendor’s policy position. Whether that’s because a vendor might not strictly adhere to its policies or can change policies anytime without notice, it doesn’t matter.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: What you need to know about Python 3.14’s faster interpreter
item.description: Python 3.14, due out later this year, is set to receive a new type of interpreter that can boost performance by up to 30% with no changes to existing code. The CPython 3.14 change log describes the feature as “a new type of interpreter based on tail calls.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Musk furious as judge shuts down DOGE access to Treasury payment system
item.description: The US Treasury Department’s payment servers hold the tax returns, social security data and bank account numbers of every adult citizen of the United States.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: FPGAs lose luster in genAI era
item.description: Intel invested $16.7 billion when it bought FPGA maker Altera in 2016, while AMD spent upwards of $35 billion in 2020 to acquire Xilinx, but the vendors have little if anything to show for it.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Ye's X account deleted after antisemitic posts
item.description: Ye, the rapper formerly known as Kanye West, has left the social media platform X after a days-long posting spree that included multiple antisemitic comments.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Tech unemployment jumps, even as job openings increase
item.description: The unemployment rate among tech workers leaped almost a full percentage point from December to January, according to an analysis of US Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS) data released Friday.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Trump's AI ambition and China's DeepSeek overshadow an AI summit in Paris
item.description: PARIS (AP) — The geopolitics of artificial intelligence will be in focus at a major summit in France where world leaders, executives and experts will hammer out pledges on guiding the development of the rapidly advancing technology.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIE CORBET and KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Top 20 apps tracking you every day
item.description: The apps collecting the most data about you are among the most widely used. Kurt the CyberGuy takes a look at 20 of these apps and how you can protect personal information.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Latino workers working to overcome a technological divide brought on by automation, AI
item.description: As jobs become more reliant on technology some Latino workers can be left behind due to a lack of digital skills exacerbated by a lack of accessibility.
item.dc:creator: FERNANDA FIGUEROA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mistral releases its genAI assistant Le Chat for IOS and Android
item.description: French AI company Mistral has released several updates to its generative AI assistant Le Chat and made it available on Android and iOS.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: How the slash-and-burn tactics Musk brought to Washington backfired at Twitter
item.description: When Elon Musk bought Twitter in 2022, he laid off thousands of employees, stopped paying rent and auctioned off coffee makers and office chairs in hopes of a big turnaround.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN and BERNARD CONDON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Amazon to pay nearly $4M to settle lawsuit alleging it took tips from drivers
item.description: Amazon has agreed to pay nearly $4 million to settle charges that the e-commerce company subsidized its labor costs by taking tips its delivery drivers received from customers, District of Columbia Attorney General Brian L. Schwalb said Friday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: OpenAI looks across US for sites to build its Trump-backed Stargate AI data centers
item.description: OpenAI is scouring the U.S. for sites to build a network of huge data centers to power its artificial intelligence technology, expanding beyond a flagship Texas location and looking across 16 states to accelerate the Stargate project championed by President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Beware of fake Reddit solutions delivering dangerous malware
item.description: Bad actors are now mimicking Reddit to spread malware that can steal personal information. CyberGuy shares what you need to know about fake Reddit pages.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: One Tech Tip: How to block your phone from tracking your location
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Smartphones are useful tools for everyday life, but they're privy to nearly everything about you, including all the places you've been — if you let them.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Google scraps its diversity hiring goals as it complies with Trump's new government contractor rules
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Google is scrapping some of its diversity hiring targets, joining a lengthening list of U.S. companies that have abandoned or scaled back their diversity, equity and inclusion programs.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Man jailed over police AI program, then freed 17 months after victim raised doubts
item.description: A grainy surveillance photo of an assault suspect on St. Louis' Metrolink led a computer facial recognition program to identify Chris Gatlin.
item.dc:creator: Chris Hayes, NewsNation

item.title: Apple lashes out at iPhone porn app maker and the EU rules allowing its download
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Apple has chided a newly introduced pornography app available in the European Union, warning that the bloc’s digital rules opening the way for third-party app store downloads undermines consumer confidence in the tech giant.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, NewsNation
